column1: # grep-tells.tsv — TIER 1 (flat-presence) rules for audit-swiftui-animation-motion (anim-01 … anim-11).
# severity: hard | warn | adv   (hard → runner exits 2). The runner greps each ERE over the target *.swift.
# This LOCATES only — the LLM agent READS each hit (SKILL.md step 3) and decides. Structural rules grep
# cannot express (argument arity, namespace identity, gate scope) live in ast-grep/*.yml (tier 2).

column4: .animation( present — confirm it is the value-scoped .animation(_:value:); a single-arg .animation(x) is the DEPRECATED implicit form (macOS 12) → add value: or use withAnimation (anim-01). ast-grep anim-01 refines to single-arg.
withAnimation — confirm its body mutates @State/observed the view actually reads; a no-op body or a constant value: animates nothing (anim-02).

spring preset (.bouncy/.smooth/.snappy) — macOS 14 despite the rendered 10.15 (DocC quirk). REPORT only if deployment target < macOS 14 and ungated; gate #available(macOS 14, *) (anim-04). Read floor in ORIENT.
raw duration curve — for physical/interactive motion a spring preset .bouncy/.smooth/.snappy reads more native (anim-03, judgment/flag).
hand-rolled / repeating loop — multi-step motion may fit PhaseAnimator/KeyframeAnimator (macOS 14) better (anim-05).
.symbolEffect — macOS 14.0+. REPORT if floor < 14 and ungated (gate #available(macOS 14,*)); else confirm a hand-animated SF Symbol shouldn't use it (anim-06).
contentTransition present — confirm value changes (numeric Text, swapped symbol) use it; its ABSENCE on an animated value is the defect (anim-07, READ the value change).
matchedGeometryEffect — verify ONE shared @Namespace + matching id + single withAnimation transaction + one isSource (anim-08).
matchedGeometryEffect on glassed views — if both matched views carry .glassEffect(), morph with glassEffectID in a GlassEffectContainer instead (anim-09, cross_ref liquid-glass).
continuous/large motion — confirm it reads @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) and has a reduced path (anim-10, cross_ref accessibility).
always-on motion — UX-restraint verdict here; render-cost verdict is view-performance (anim-11, cross_ref view-performance).
